title: Bitcoin Miner Bitfarms Sinks to Fourth-Quarter Loss as Difficulty, Costs Rise
text: Bitfarms reported Q4 2022 net loss due to rising Bitcoin mining difficulty and increasing operational costs. The quarter showed $27 million revenue with $17 million net loss or $(0.08) per share. Bitcoin mining average cost increased to $11,100 per BTC as network difficulty rose 20% during the period. Company operated 4.5 EH/s of hashrate, with 13% hashrate increase offset by lower Bitcoin prices (down 15%) and increased network difficulty. The challenging Q4 2022 environment reflected broader Bitcoin mining industry headwinds following crypto market downturn. Adjusted EBITDA contracted to $1 million (4% margin) from $10 million (31% margin) in prior quarter, highlighting margin compression from cost inflation exceeding revenue growth. For full year 2022, Bitfarms mined 5,167 BTC at average production cost of $10,000 per BTC. Annual revenue declined 16% to $142 million with net loss of $239 million. Management commentary addressed cost management strategies and operational optimization. The report detailed facility shutdown decisions and asset write-downs reflecting operational challenges in challenging market conditions.
